name: Prerna
introCommands: git commit -m "lost to a chartered accountant"
taglinePhrases: makes discipline look like an art form.
carries a quiet confidence that commands rooms.
turns ambition into something effortlessly graceful.
balances entire worlds without losing her composure.
walked into my story and rewrote the whole thing.
description: Future Chartered Accountant with the kind of quiet discipline most people only talk about. Sharp, composed, and effortlessly grounded — someone who makes everyone around her want to do better. Including me. Especially me.
greeting: this has been in my heart's cache for a while ✨

terminal.secretLine1: confession = "the thing I never said out loud";

message:
I sometimes think about who I was before you — and honestly, that life doesn't even compare. You changed the way I feel, the way I see the world, and the way I understand what love truly means.

You didn't just come into my life — you gave it a direction it didn't know it was missing.

I don't want something temporary with you. I don't want something that only works when it's easy. I want something real, something honest, something that only grows stronger with every season we walk through together. I want us — not for now, not for a while — for life.

I love you, Prerna ❤️
from: — with all my love, today and always 💜
hint: each one holds something I never said out loud
notes: you became my favorite constant — not the loudest part of my life, but the one that quietly makes everything else make sense
there's a quiet brilliance in the way you carry yourself — unhurried, unfazed, and completely unmissable
you brought a rhythm to my days I didn't know was missing — everything just flows better with you in it
the way you show up for your CA every single day — that kind of discipline is rare, and watching it made me realize I wasn't just impressed, I was already falling
not sure when it happened, but you went from someone on my mind to someone I can't imagine my life without
knowing you feel it too — that quiet certainty is the most peaceful thing I've ever known
